steps.question: Why are XML-style tags (like <context> ... </context>) useful in a prompt?
options: They make the model respond faster.
They clearly separate different parts of the input so the model can tell them apart.
They are required for the API to accept the request.
They reduce the number of tokens used.
steps.explanation: Tags delimit sections such as instructions, context, and examples so the model can distinguish them. They are a helpful convention, not an API requirement.

steps.task: Write a prompt that gives Claude a block of user-provided text inside tags and asks it to list any spelling mistakes.
steps.modelAnswer:
Find every spelling mistake in the text below and list each one on its own line. Only consider the text inside the tags:

<text>
{{USER_TEXT}}
</text>
hints: Wrap the user text in a clearly named tag.
Tell the model to only look inside the tags.
